metadata.title: Detox — Understand smartphone addiction
metadata.description: An interactive video experience and a scientifically validated questionnaire to help you understand your relationship with your smartphone.

hero.title: Understand your relationship with your smartphone
hero.subtitle: An interactive video experience and a scientifically validated questionnaire, designed with and for Swiss schools.
hero.ctaSecondary: Or draw a 'Put down the phone' card →

pillars.title: Seven pillars, one coherent approach
pillars.subtitle: Every project decision ties back to at least one of these axes.

durable.title: Durable
durable.description: GDPR compliance, maintainable code, open-source questionnaire.

addiction.title: Addiction
addiction.description: Solid scientific grounding (SAS-SV scale), responsible narrative.

collaboration.title: Collaboration
collaboration.description: Dashboard for teachers, class-level aggregated data.

interdisciplinaire.title: Interdisciplinary
interdisciplinaire.description: Psychology, UX, public health and data science combined.

accessible.title: Accessible
accessible.description: WCAG 2.1 AA, keyboard navigation, screen readers.

footer.tagline: Detox — an HEFP project for youth digital health.
footer.gdpr: Your answers are anonymous. See our {policy}.
footer.rights: © 2026 Detox project. Open-source parts under MIT licence.

consent.title: Before you start
consent.body: To improve the experience, we record 100% anonymous data (answers, video interactions). No personal information is collected. Your data is never sold.

challenges.title: Put down the phone
challenges.subtitle: Draw a card. Do the thing. Come back and tell us if you did it. No grade, no judgement.

curieux.label: Curious
curieux.subtitle: You're exploring, and you like it.

habitue.label: Regular
habitue.subtitle: Putting the phone down without thinking.

full_circle.title: Full circle
full_circle.description: At least one card from every category.

marathon.title: Marathon
marathon.description: 10 cards completed in a single day.

meal_phone_free.title: Phone-free meal
meal_phone_free.description: You finished a meal without your phone.

night_owl.title: Night owl
night_owl.description: A card finished between 10 pm and 2 am.

early_bird.title: Early bird
early_bird.description: A card finished between 5 am and 7 am.

programs.title: Programs
programs.subtitle: Themed sequences of cards, designed to build a habit.

matin_sans_ecran.title: Screen-free morning
matin_sans_ecran.description: 5 cards to start the day gently.

connexions.title: Connections
connexions.description: 10 cards to strengthen the bonds around you.

bouge_ton_corps.title: Move your body
bouge_ton_corps.description: 7 cards to move a little each day.

sept_jours_calme.title: Seven days of calm
sept_jours_calme.description: 7 cards to slow down and breathe.

metadata.title: My profile — Put down the phone
My certificate — Put down the phone

certificate.headline: Put down the phone
certificate.quote: You put the phone down {n} times. Each one was a choice.
certificate.noProgramsYet: No programs finished yet — that's OK.

active.label: Active card
active.hint: Put the phone down for about {min, plural, one {# minute} other {# minutes}}. Come back when you're done.

done.title: Nice work.
done.body: You did {completed, plural, =0 {no cards} one {# card} other {# cards}} and skipped {skipped, plural, =0 {none} one {# time} other {# times}}. Come back tomorrow — there will still be cards.

window_describe.title: At the window
window_describe.body: Go to a window. Describe out loud, in two sentences, what you see.

five_sounds.title: Five sounds
five_sounds.body: Close your eyes. Count five different sounds around you.

count_colors.title: Six colours
count_colors.body: Find six objects of different colours in the room.

stranger_outfit.title: Stranger's outfit
stranger_outfit.body: The next person you cross paths with: silently note three things they're wearing.

sky_minute.title: One minute of sky
sky_minute.body: Step outside or look out a window. Watch the sky for one minute, doing nothing else.

object_history.title: The oldest object
object_history.body: Find the oldest object near you. Imagine its story.

ask_day.title: How was your day?
ask_day.body: Ask someone nearby how their day went. Really listen.

compliment_someone.title: A compliment
compliment_someone.body: Give someone a sincere compliment — not on their looks, on something they did.

phone_call_friend.title: A phone call
phone_call_friend.body: Call someone (yes, by voice, not by message). Just to say hi.

thank_someone.title: Thank you
thank_someone.body: Thank someone — a parent, a friend, a teacher — for something specific.

ask_advice.title: Some advice
ask_advice.body: Ask someone older for advice on anything. Note what they say.

share_meal_no_phone.title: Phone-free meal
share_meal_no_phone.body: Your next meal — table, chair, phone in another room. Eat with conversation or in silence.

ten_squats.title: Ten squats
ten_squats.body: Stand up and do ten squats. Count out loud if it helps.

stretch_one_minute.title: Stretch
stretch_one_minute.body: One minute to stretch whatever hurts — neck, shoulders, back.

walk_around_block.title: Walk the block
walk_around_block.body: Step outside for a ten-minute walk. No earbuds, no phone in hand.

stairs_three_times.title: Stairs, three times
stairs_three_times.body: Go up and down a flight of stairs three times. Breathe well.

dance_one_song.title: One song, standing
dance_one_song.body: Put on a song you love. Stand up. Move until it ends.

barefoot_outside.title: Barefoot outside
barefoot_outside.body: If you can, walk barefoot on grass or earth for five minutes.

draw_what_you_see.title: Draw what you see
draw_what_you_see.body: Take paper and pen. Draw an object in front of you. It doesn't need to be pretty.

write_haiku.title: A haiku
write_haiku.body: Write three lines: 5 syllables, 7 syllables, 5 syllables. About anything.

fold_paper_thing.title: Fold something
fold_paper_thing.body: Take a sheet of paper. Fold a plane, a frog, a flower — what you know or improvise.

rearrange_desk.title: Tidy your desk
rearrange_desk.body: Ten minutes to tidy your work space. Throw out what's useless.

letter_to_future_me.title: Letter to one-year-from-now you
letter_to_future_me.body: Write a letter to yourself a year from now. Three things you want to remember.

humming_a_tune.title: Hum a tune
humming_a_tune.body: Hum a melody for two minutes. Made-up or not — doesn't matter.

eyes_closed_minute.title: Eyes closed
eyes_closed_minute.body: One minute, eyes closed. Don't try not to think. Just sit.

three_good_things.title: Three good things
three_good_things.body: Note (in your head or on paper) three things that went well today, even small ones.

breathing_4_7_8.title: 4-7-8 breathing
breathing_4_7_8.body: Inhale 4 seconds, hold 7, exhale 8. Four cycles.

imagine_summer.title: Summer memory
imagine_summer.body: Close your eyes. Picture one moment from this summer you remember well.

what_will_matter.title: Ten years from now
what_will_matter.body: Think about what you're doing today. What will still matter in ten years?

drink_water_slowly.title: A glass, slowly
drink_water_slowly.body: Pour yourself a glass of water. Drink it doing nothing else. Feel each sip.

scan_room_details.title: Details around you
scan_room_details.body: Find three small details you'd never noticed before.

smile_at_stranger.title: A smile
smile_at_stranger.body: Smile at the next person whose eyes meet yours. No words needed.

shoulder_rolls.title: Shoulder rolls
shoulder_rolls.body: Ten slow rolls backward, then ten forward. Subtle, works anywhere.

invent_a_story.title: Invent a story
invent_a_story.body: Pick someone around you (without them noticing). Imagine their day.

notice_one_feeling.title: Name what you feel
notice_one_feeling.body: Ask yourself: what am I feeling right now? Find one precise word.

lines.3: The phone buzzes once, then again.
lines.4: Three new messages. An Insta notification. A live on TikTok.

options.B: I flip it face-down and start studying.

lines.2: Video, video, video. A friend in a story.
lines.3: You reply quickly. You scroll some more.
lines.4: 10:13 PM. You put the phone down. The math test is in eleven hours.

lines.1: 8:47 PM. The phone is face-down on the desk.
lines.3: A notification flashes. You hesitate for a second.
lines.4: 9:35 PM. You close the book. You feel a little tired — but ready.

choice_2.prompt: Morning, at the bus stop. Your friends show you a meme. The phone buzzes. How do you want to spend the next twenty minutes?

options.A: I pull out my phone too — it's easier.
options.B: I leave it in my pocket and listen.

lines.2: The three of you laugh at the same video.
lines.3: No-one is really looking at each other.
lines.4: The bus arrives. You didn't hear what Léa was saying.

lines.1: The phone buzzes. You feel it.
lines.2: Léa tells you about an argument with her sister.
lines.3: You ask a question. She answers at length.
lines.4: The bus arrives. You know things about her life you didn't know yesterday.

lines.1: None of these stories is "the right one".
lines.2: But each small choice shapes your relationship with the phone.
lines.3: We'll ask you ten questions to better understand yours.

closing.title: And you?
closing.body: None of these stories is "the right one". Each small choice shapes your relationship with the phone. We'll ask you ten questions to better understand yours.

survey.title: SAS-SV questionnaire
survey.subtitle: 10 statements about your smartphone use. Pick the number that fits you best. There are no right or wrong answers — be honest with yourself.
survey.intro: For each statement: 1 = strongly disagree, 6 = strongly agree. Your answers are anonymous and saved as you go — you can close the page and resume later.

results.title: Your results
results.instrumentNote: Pre-validated French adaptation of the SAS-SV (Kwon et al., 2013). Independent validation in progress — see methodology.

low.heading: Moderate use
low.body: Your score suggests your smartphone has a measured place in your day. Keep noticing how you feel when you put it down.
low.reflect: A question for you: what do you enjoy most about moments without a smartphone?

moderate.heading: Use to watch
moderate.body: Your score is in an intermediate zone. Several habits deserve attention — no alarm, but stay mindful.
moderate.reflect: A question for you: if you had to cut your screen time by one hour a day, what would you start with?

high.heading: Heavy use
high.body: Your score indicates heavy smartphone use, with possible effects on focus, sleep or relationships. This doesn't define who you are — it's a signal to open a conversation.
high.reflect: A question for you: who that you trust could you talk to this week about your smartphone use?

symptoms.title: Breakdown by dimension
symptoms.subtitle: Each bar reflects a different aspect of your smartphone relationship.

resources.title: Support resources
resources.subtitle: If you want to talk, these Swiss services are free and confidential.

proJuventute.name: Pro Juventute · 147
proJuventute.description: 24/7 advice by phone, chat or SMS, free and anonymous.

promotionSante.name: Promotion Santé Suisse
promotionSante.description: Public foundation for prevention and mental health.

stopAddiction.name: Addiction Suisse
stopAddiction.description: Information and support around addictions, including digital ones.

results.disclaimer: This questionnaire is a personal reflection tool, not a medical diagnosis. If you are concerned, consult a healthcare professional.

errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
